successCriteria.description: src/modules/leave/leave.model.ts exists and exports LeaveType, LeaveRequestStatus, and LeaveRequest with fields id, employeeId, leaveType, startDate, endDate, status, approverEmployeeId, and createdAt.
src/modules/leave/leave.repository.ts exists and exports a LeaveRepository interface with create, findById, and findByEmployeeId methods operating on LeaveRequest types imported from leave.model.ts.
src/modules/leave/postgres-leave.repository.ts exists and exports PostgresLeaveRepository implementing LeaveRepository using a pg Pool.
A PostgreSQL migration exists that creates the leave_requests table with the specified columns, constraints, and indexes from the provided schema.
Tests verify repository persistence behavior: create a LeaveRequest, retrieve it by id, and list requests by employeeId, while using the canonical LeaveRequest types.

constraints: Use the repository pattern: all database access must be encapsulated behind the LeaveRepository interface and its implementation.
The repository and persistence implementation must use LeaveRequest types defined in src/modules/leave/leave.model.ts as the canonical model source.
All async repository operations must catch and handle errors; no unhandled promise rejections.
No sensitive data may be logged by repository code.

Audit records are required for state-changing operations by platform standards; any implemented persistence create operation must remain compatible with audit-record writing requirements, even if full audit functionality is not introduced in this phase.
Keep scope limited to leave domain models, repository contracts/implementation, persistence schema, and repository tests.
outOfScope: Leave approval and rejection workflow logic.
API endpoints, request validation, and RBAC endpoint enforcement.
Any leave features beyond model definitions, repository boundaries, PostgreSQL persistence, and related tests.

ambiguities.description: The intent requests 'DTOs used by persistence' in leave.model.ts, but the architecture definition only specifies LeaveType, LeaveRequestStatus, and LeaveRequest.
options: Define only the explicitly specified types/interfaces from the architecture definition.
Add additional persistence DTO types alongside LeaveRequest.

ambiguities.description: The intent explicitly mentions a unit test file, while the architecture success criteria describe behavior that typically requires database-backed repository verification.
options: Implement integration-style repository tests (potentially in the specified file) that verify PostgreSQL persistence behavior.
